title: Playbook - Power BI Samples, Prototyping, And Report Bootstrapping
applies_to: Power BI learning, prototyping, and initial report design using official samples

confirmed_facts.fact: Official Power BI samples are available as built-in samples, downloadable PBIX files, Excel workbooks, templates, and direct database samples.
A PBIX file contains the report pages, data model, queries, and visuals and is intended for Power BI Desktop.
An XLSX sample workbook can be used as a data source in Power BI Desktop or published from Excel to the Power BI service, and advanced Excel users can inspect or edit the model in Power Pivot.
The samples page explicitly recommends using official samples to try features, prototype reports, validate scenarios, or jumpstart projects.

implementation_notes: Use sample PBIX files when the goal is to learn report structure, visual design, page navigation, and semantic-model layout in one artifact.
Use sample XLSX files when the goal is to practice source ingestion or compare Excel-originated models with Power BI-authored models.
Treat official samples as scaffolding, not production architecture. Replace sample source assumptions, measures, and visuals with domain-specific equivalents before calling a design production-ready.
When building knowledge or implementation guides, samples are most useful for naming concrete starting artifacts and showing the difference between report design and data-source design.
caveats: Samples are educational accelerators, not evidence that a production data model or refresh design is correct.
A report that works well with a sample PBIX may still require a very different semantic model when connected to real enterprise data.
